text: The surgeon resects the affected segment of the colon, followed by the resection of the mesocolon at both proximal and distal ends. Using ICG control, the section area is marked. The anvil is then removed from the back and introduced through a colotomy into the healthy colon near the knot area.
The surgeon uses a six-tip blue surform to section the affected segment of the colon at the proximal and distal ends. The resected segment is placed into a bag for later extraction.

The surgeon extracts the anvil through the terminal part of the proximal colon, securing a reinforced pull-string in the distal part. After boosting, the proximal part of the anvil is extracted under visual control. A mechanical end-to-end anastomosis is performed as part of the left colectomy procedure.
